title: Mehrangarh Fort
tagline: Mehrangarh Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Mehrangarh is a historic fort located in Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India.
overview.description: Mehrangarh is a historic fort located in Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. It stands on a hilltop, rising about 122 m (400 ft) above the surrounding plains, and the complex spans 1,200 acres (486 hectares). It was initially built around 1459 by the Rajput ruler of Rathore clan Rao Jodha, though most of the existing structure is from the 17th century built by his successors. The fort has seven gates, including the main entrance, Jai Pol (meaning 'victory gate'), built by Maharaja Man Singh to commemorate his victories over the Jaipur and Bikaner armies in 1806.

topPlaces.name: Ghanta Ghar (Jodhpur)
topPlaces.description: Ghanta Ghar, also known as the clock tower of Rajasthan, is in the Indian city of Jodhpur.

topPlaces.name: Rao Jodha Desert Rock Park
topPlaces.description: Rao Jodha Desert Rock Park, spreads over 72 hectares, near the historic Mehrangarh Fort in Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. The park contains ecologically restored desert and arid land vegetation.

topPlaces.name: Jaswant Thada
topPlaces.description: The Jaswant Thada is a cenotaph located in Jodhpur, in the Indian state of Rajasthan. It was built by Maharaja Sardar Singh of Jodhpur State in 1899 in memory of his father, Maharaja Jaswant Singh II, and serves as the cremation ground for the royal Rajput family of Marwar.

topPlaces.name: Maharaja Umaid Singh Stadium
topPlaces.description: Maharaja Umaid Singh Stadium, formerly known as the Umaid Sports Stadium, is a multipurpose stadium situated in the Paota suburb of Jodhpur, Rajasthan.The sports complex is built in 1932 headed by Maharaja of Jodhpur Umaid Singh as a sports ground. It is mostly used for political rallies, cultural activities and sporting events.

topPlaces.name: Sardar Government Museum
topPlaces.description: The Sardar Government Museum (also known as Jodhpur Government Museum, Jodhpur State Museum or Museum Jodhpur) is located in Public Park (Ummed Bagh), Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. The museum is named in memory of Maharaja Sardar Singh, a ruler of Jodhpur from 1895 to 1911.

topPlaces.name: Jodhpur Group – Malani Igneous Suite Contact
topPlaces.description: Jodhpur Group-Malani Igneous Suite Contact of Aravalli range is a geological feature representing the last phase of igneous activity of Precambrian age in the Indian subcontinent at the foot of the picturesque Mehrangarh Fort in Jodhpur city, the second largest city in Rajasthan after Jaipur. The uniqueness of the geological feature at Jodhpur prompted the Geological Survey of India (GSI) to declare the site as a National Geological Monument.

topPlaces.name: Jodhpur
topPlaces.description: Jodhpur (Hindi pronunciation: [ˈd͡ʒoːd̪ʱ.pʊr] ) is the second-largest city of the north-western Indian state of Rajasthan, after its capital Jaipur. As of 2025, the city has a population of 1.6 million.

topPlaces.name: Sardarpura
topPlaces.description: Sardarpura is a village of the Tehsil Rajgarh (Rajasthan). It is situated at Latitude (28.524813), Longitude (75.512563) or +28° 31' 29.33" North, +75° 30' 45.23 East.

It is mostly used for political rallies, cultural activities and sporting events.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mehrangarh Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mehrangarh Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mehrangarh Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jodhpur Airport (6 km) and the nearest railway station is Jodhpur Junction (1 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mehrangarh Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mehrangarh Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mehrangarh Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Mehrangarh Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mehrangarh Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ghanta Ghar (Jodhpur), Rao Jodha Desert Rock Park, Jaswant Thada, Maharaja Umaid Singh Stadium, Sardar Government Museum.
